install.summary: Open-source AI agent by Nous Research (MIT). ONE installer builds the Electron desktop GUI AND puts the `hermes` CLI on PATH — same repo, not two products.
install.windows_official: Global / unblocked networks: download Hermes-Setup.exe from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop and run it.
install.macos_official: Global / unblocked networks: download the .dmg from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop.
install.linux_official: Global / unblocked networks: follow the terminal install at https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop.
install.note: NOT a click-through wizard like Claude Desktop. The official installer is a thin BOOTSTRAPPER that fetches scripts/install.ps1 (Windows) or install.sh (mac/Linux) from raw.githubusercontent.com, then runs a heavy ~16-step build: uv -> Python 3.11 -> Git -> Node.js 22 -> clone repo -> Python/Node deps -> Electron desktop build -> Playwright Chromium -> PATH. In mainland China the bootstrapper dies at the very first hop (raw.githubusercontent.com is GFW-blocked, os error 10054 / RST), so the agent must DRIVE a mirrored install — see install_flow.agent_steps.

cli.note: The same install also yields a `hermes` CLI on PATH (oneshot: `hermes chat -q "<message>" --quiet`). Primary user-facing form is the desktop GUI; the CLI is a bonus, not separately maintained.

install_flow.summary: Probe network. Global/reachable -> run the official installer. Mainland China (github/raw blocked) -> bypass the bootstrapper and run the install script via a reachable GitHub mirror, with China package mirrors set as env vars.
agent_steps: Desktop app — LOCAL machine only. If connected to a remote server, switch to the local server first.

NETWORK PROBE: test whether raw.githubusercontent.com and github.com are reachable (e.g. `curl -m 5 -sI https://raw.githubusercontent.com`). If reachable (global / unblocked) -> run the OFFICIAL installer directly (Windows: download & run Hermes-Setup.exe from hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/desktop; macOS: the .dmg; Linux: the official terminal install) and SKIP the mirror steps. Keep this path working for global users.

IF raw/github are blocked (mainland China — the common case): do NOT run Hermes-Setup.exe (its first hop to raw.githubusercontent.com fails with os error 10054 / RST). Drive a mirrored install instead, steps A-D below.

STEP A — set China package mirrors as environment variables for the whole install session (these npmmirror/Tsinghua mirrors are stable and were verified to exist): PyPI -> `UV_DEFAULT_INDEX=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple` and `PIP_INDEX_URL=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple`; uv's Python download -> `UV_PYTHON_INSTALL_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/python-build-standalone`; npm -> `npm_config_registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com`; Node.js portable -> npmmirror node mirror `https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/node/`; Electron -> `ELECTRON_MIRROR=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/electron/`; Playwright -> `PLAYWRIGHT_DOWNLOAD_HOST=https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/playwright`.

STEP B — route git through a reachable GitHub proxy so the repo clone (and any github.com release downloads) succeed: `git config --global url."https://<working-gh-proxy>/https://github.com/".insteadOf "https://github.com/"`. GitHub proxies (ghproxy-class) are volatile — pick one that currently responds. For PortableGit specifically you can instead use npmmirror's `git-for-windows` mirror (https://registry.npmmirror.com/-/binary/git-for-windows/) or just pre-install Git (`winget install Git.Git`).

STEP C — fetch the install SCRIPT from a China-reachable GitHub mirror instead of the blocked raw.githubusercontent.com: Windows `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/scripts/install.ps1`, mac/Linux the same path ending `/install.sh`. jsdelivr mirrors GitHub raw and was verified to serve this file; if jsdelivr is unreachable on this network, use a ghproxy-class raw proxy.

STEP D — run the fetched script with the STEP A env vars set and the STEP B git proxy configured. The script's internal github.com calls are redirected by the git insteadOf rule; its pip / npm / node / electron / playwright downloads are redirected by the env vars.

BOUNDED LATITUDE + SECURITY: mirrors (jsdelivr / gh-proxy) come and go — if a hop fails, find an alternative working mirror at runtime rather than giving up; you may improvise beyond these steps. BUT the script you run is the OFFICIAL Hermes repo content fetched via a mirror — make sure the mirror serves the genuine repo (do not run a modified/untrusted script), and NEVER recommend a specific commercial VPN.
VERIFY: after install, `hermes --version` confirms the CLI is on PATH; the Electron desktop app is built under %LOCALAPPDATA%\hermes (Windows) / the app bundle (macOS).
Tell the user plainly what was installed (Hermes desktop GUI + `hermes` CLI), where, and how to launch it.
tell_user: Whether the official or the China-mirrored path was used
That ONE install gives both the desktop GUI and the `hermes` CLI
Install location and how to launch
If a non-default mirror/proxy was used, that it was a China-network workaround

blocked_regions.zh-CN: The official Hermes-Setup.exe is a bootstrapper that fetches scripts/install.ps1 from raw.githubusercontent.com — ~always GFW-blocked in mainland China (os error 10054 / RST), so the official installer fails outright. The build also pulls from github.com (clone, PortableGit), PyPI, nodejs.org, npm, the Electron CDN and the Playwright CDN — most are throttled/blocked in CN. Bypass: fetch the script via jsdelivr/ghproxy and set China package mirrors (Tsinghua PyPI; npmmirror for node/npm/electron/playwright/python-build-standalone; a GitHub proxy for the git clone). See install_flow.agent_steps.

mirrors.github_raw: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/NousResearch/hermes-agent@main/ (jsdelivr; or a ghproxy-class raw proxy, volatile — verify at runtime)
mirrors.github_clone: git insteadOf via a ghproxy-class proxy (volatile — verify at runtime)
